rows.notes: Very clear centred page title '1881' at top of page. High contrast and unambiguous.
Clearly written heading under the date. Underline present; no amounts on this line.

Date and surname 'June 15 Penny' are legible in left margin. The large pounds figure '450' aligned with this top entry is clear; shillings '13' and pence '1' readable though the '1' pence is slightly faint at the edge.
Description 'Thatching' clear in centre column. Amount columns show '3' in pounds column, '9' shillings and '6' pence; digits well aligned with vertical ruling lines.
'Mason' clearly written. Pound figure '2' and '3' shillings are distinct; pence appears as '0' (blank pence column) and alignment with column lines supports this reading.
Description appears as 'Slags' (handwriting ambiguous between 'Slagg'/'Slags'). Amounts read as 2 pounds, 2 shillings; pence column blank. Moderate uncertainty due to handwriting of description.
'Drainage Rate' legible. Pounds '1' and shillings '9' align clearly; pence blank. Slight ink variation on 'Drainage' but amount columns unambiguous.
Name 'Harris' clear. Pounds '6', shillings '5' and pence '0' read cleanly from the ruled columns.
'Quit Rent' readable. Pounds '28' is distinct; shillings and pence are both zero/blank. Slight smudge on the line makes shillings/pence faint but likely zero.
Surname looks like 'Norridge' (handwriting ambiguous). Associated pounds '10' visible; smaller columns for shillings/pence blank. Moderate uncertainty on exact spelling of name.
Handwritten name interpreted as 'Emberlin' (letters uncertain). Pounds '12' and shillings '5' readable; pence blank. Moderate uncertainty in name reading.
Entry reads 'Scroggs' (handwriting loop on final letters). Pounds '6' and shillings '5' visible but slightly fainter than previous rows; pence blank.
Short name 'Hill' readable. Pounds '19' clear; pence reads as '6' though the pence digit is slightly smudged—alignment with pence column supports '6'.
Another 'Quit Rent' line. Pounds '2' and shillings '4' readable; pence appears '6' but faint—could be influenced by adjacent ink. Chosen by column alignment.
Date and description 'July 25 Jackson late Cook Donation' readable. Pounds '10' clear and shillings/pence blank. Reasonably high confidence on both description and amount.

rows.description: Augt Fisher - life Insurance payment or
rows.notes: Handwritten date abbreviation 'Augt' and name 'Fisher' followed by 'life Insurance payment or'. Pounds '12' and shillings '5' visible; pence blank. Slight ink crowding after the long description.
Description 'Yarnton Voluntary Church Rate' legible. Pounds '1', shillings '16', pence '10' read from the three small columns and pass validation (s<20, d<12).
'Faulkner Seeds' readable. Pounds '19' and shillings '10' clear; pence blank. Name reading moderate confidence due to cursive 'F'.
Short name 'Blenow' (or 'Blencow') ambiguous—letters uncertain. Amounts read as 2 pounds, 4 shillings, 6 pence; pence digit slightly faint but aligned with pence column.

Abbreviated initial 'T. Norton' and 'Chair' readable but slightly cramped. Pounds '25' read from the pounds column; shillings and pence blank. Moderate uncertainty on whether this is a subtotal or single entry.
Text appears as 'Jorden allowance to' (handwriting ambiguous); assigned pounds 29 and shillings 14 which align clearly. Pence blank. Moderate uncertainty on exact spelling of description.
Description 'Duke Domus Wine Battes' (or 'Bottles')—final word uncertain. Pounds '14' and shillings '18' clearly present; pence blank. Chosen reading based on column alignment.

Line reads 'Symmes reconstruction of clocks & Drain' followed below by 'at the back of the Rectors House'—the large amount '88 19 4' corresponds with a long reconstruction item connected to Duke/his bill area. The description is lengthy and somewhat crowded; amount columns are legible but because the description spans multiple lines confidence is moderate.

rows.description: Symmes reconstruction of clocks & drains at the back of the Rectors House
rows.notes: This long multi-line description is written across two lines; assigned pounds 23, shillings 2, pence 5 which are visible on the right. Moderate confidence due to line-wrap and ink density variations.

rows.description: Knowles new heating apparatus stoves for Rectors Hall
rows.notes: Text reads 'Knowles new heating apparatus stoves for Rectors Hall' (handwriting compressed). Amount is a small one '1 1 0' visible but slightly faint; chosen by alignment to the small pounds/shillings columns.
'Easter Offering' readable. Pounds '30' and shillings '12' are visible and aligned. Slight smudge on the shillings but the digits are distinct enough.

rows.description: Local Rate Fund see Bankers Custody
rows.notes: Description 'Local Rate Fund. see Bankers Custody' legible. Pounds '50' is clear; shillings and pence blank. There is a small handwritten note nearby but does not affect amount reading.
Line reads 'Price 1881, Pension' (somewhat cramped). Amount read as 73 pounds, 3 shillings, 9 pence; pence digit is small and slightly uncertain but aligned reasonably well.
Text appears 'Sely as Rector' or possibly 'Self as Rector' (handwriting ambiguous). Pounds '31', shillings '12', pence '6' are present but the description uncertainty lowers confidence.
'Hutt Draining' or 'Hultt Draining' reading uncertain. Amount 2 pounds, 18 shillings, 4 pence visible; shillings at the high end but <20 so valid. Slight ink bleed reduces clarity.
Single word 'Timber' legible. Amount read as 3 pounds, 3 shillings, 3 pence; small numerals a bit cramped but align with the three small columns.
'Barn Door' clear. Pounds '2' and shillings '3' visible; pence reads '6' though slightly faint—alignment supports this reading.
Short entry 'Pipes' legible. Amount '6 0 0' clearly printed in columns with good contrast.
Description 'Canal' clear. Pounds '6' and shillings '19' visible; shillings at upper limit but valid. Slight smudge around shilling digits reduces confidence.
Another 'Thatching' line appears toward the bottom. Pounds '4' and shillings '10' read from columns; description ink lighter than earlier occurrences leading to moderate confidence.
Entry 'Straw' readable though handwriting slants. Amount seen as 4 pounds, 11 shillings, 9 pence; pence digit small but legible on close inspection.

Final total at bottom right reads '4945 0 4'. The three columns are well aligned and the total is bold/clear. There is a small possibility the first digit could be '4995' but the middle digit shape matches '4' more closely; chosen as 4945 with fairly high confidence.
